subject: Re: M crashing during "precomputing gradient weights"
body:
Hi Jesse,

How many particles are there in that tomogram? I don't recall if the error
message is the same, but I've definitely had problems with large numbers of
particles on a single tomogram combined with a large box size. I can't
recall what sort of box size and particle count caused the crash, but I
believe I just increased the binning and reduced box size accordingly to
get around it.

Cheers,

Mat

On Tue, 19 Mar 2024 at 11:25, Jesse Hansen <bluufive@gmail.com> wrote:

> hi all,
>
> I am running M 1.0.9 following the WARP RELION pipeline on tilt series
> data.  Everything has seemingly been okay until now, but when I run refine
> in M using only the top two params (image warp grid and particle poses).
> See attached screenshots for refine settings.  Note I have also done image
> warp grid 3x3 and 6x4.
>
> I get the same issue when running on 1 GPU or on 4 GPUs. When I have
> terminal open while the job is running I see the error in the attached
> screenshot.   I tried excluding that tilt series in WARP then re-running M
> (Setting up from scratch) and M crashes again but on a different tilt
> series.  Repeat.  It seems like the issue may not be the tilt series but
> rather something else.
>
> thanks in advance
>
> Jesse
>
>
